headlines.title: What is happening with Keir Starmer’s leadership: At a glance
headlines.description: The prime minister is fighting to stay on in No 10 as heavy election losses trigger a Labour revolt.

headlines.title: BBC unmasks key people smuggler in network behind most small boat crossings
headlines.description: The 28-year-old Iraqi Kurd has evaded arrest by operating under the alias “Kardo Ranya”.

headlines.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
headlines.description: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

headlines.title: Green Party admits Polanski may have failed to pay council tax
headlines.description: The Green Party admits that its leader has, until recently, been living on a houseboat in London and may have failed to pay council tax.

headlines.title: How driving test booking is changing for learner drivers
headlines.description: From 12 May, only learner drivers can book their own tests, not instructors.

headlines.title: US in closely guarded talks to open new bases in Greenland
headlines.description: It is seeking to open three bases in the south of the Arctic territory, according to multiple officials familiar with the talks.

headlines.title: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
headlines.description: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.

headlines.title: How the Trump-Xi summit could set superpower relations for many years to come
headlines.description: Trump's whirlwind visit to China is the most significant for years - but will it bear fruit?

headlines.title: Calls for 'urgent action' on baby-sleep industry after BBC investigation
headlines.description: The BBC investigation revealed how some self-described sleep experts have been giving new parents advice that goes against NHS guidelines.

headlines.title: Zoe Ball confirms she did not get Strictly presenting role
headlines.description: The broadcaster says she was "so chuffed to even be in the mix" for the job on the BBC One show.

headlines.title: Japanese snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
headlines.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.

headlines.title: Why a slice of Edinburgh is being bought up by overseas owners
headlines.description: More than a third of the properties in Quartermile area have been bought by people based outside the UK.

headlines.title: 'Patients have tried to punch me because of my skin colour'
headlines.description: Figures from some NHS trusts in England show there was a 17% rise in reports of racism in a year.

headlines.title: Russia's shadow fleet ships defying PM's threat and entering UK waters
headlines.description: BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests "shadow fleet" vessels sailed into UK water despite the government threatening to board them.

headlines.title: I led hikers up an Indonesian volcano - and then it erupted
headlines.description: An Indonesian guide who led hikers up Mount Dukono has described the moment of Friday's eruption which killed three people.

headlines.title: Baby loss couple: We were told we'd picked a bad day to give birth
headlines.description: A couple whose baby was stillborn in hospital hope a maternity review will lead to improvements.

headlines.title: How are countries responding to hantavirus?
headlines.description: The UK, US and EU are asking all citizens returning home from the virus-hit MV Hondius to self-isolate for about six weeks.

headlines.title: 'Superhuman' managers shouldn't have to ask for help - Family of ex-Liverpool boss on loss
headlines.description: The family of former WSL manager Matt Beard tell BBC Sport about the emotional burden of football management, and the changes they want to see.

headlines.title: Students at risk if universities go bust, say MPs
headlines.description: An Education Select Committee report finds the government needs to make urgent plans for universities facing insolvency.

headlines.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
headlines.description: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

headlines.title: Texas accuses Netflix of spying on users, including children
headlines.description: The lawsuit comes amid increased scrutiny over platform features like auto-play that deliver endless content to users.

headlines.title: Complaints against judge to be examined in U-turn
headlines.description: A watchdog says it will examine complaints against employment tribunal judge Philip Lancaster.

headlines.title: Israel passes law to allow death penalty and public trials for those linked to 7 October attacks
headlines.description: Israel passes a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for those involved in the unprecedented Hamas-led attacks.

headlines.title: Indigenous Australians win record A$150m after billionaire mined without permission
headlines.description: The Yindjibarndi people had sought A$1.8bn compensation for cultural damage and economic loss.

headlines.title: China should stop hoarding food and fertiliser, says former World Bank chief
headlines.description: David Malpass also said that Beijing's claim to be a developing nation was no longer credible.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Labour MPs Move Against Starmer
headlines.description: The number of Labour MPs calling for the prime minister to go increases.

headlines.title: Trump takes his revenge on disloyal Republicans
headlines.description: The US president targets his political enemies in Kentucky and Indiana

headlines.title: Scottish mockumentary about two brothers' Eurovision dreams
headlines.description: Two Scottish brothers try to sneak into Eurovision by entering for another country.

headlines.title: Spurs drag out safety battle to keep nerves jangling
headlines.description: Tottenham hit the self-destruct button once more to leave Roberto de Zerbi frustrated as fight to avoid relegation looks set to go to the wire, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: 'Superhuman' managers shouldn't have to ask for help - Beard's family on loss
headlines.description: The family of former WSL manager Matt Beard tell BBC Sport about the emotional burden of football management, and the changes they want to see.

headlines.title: Archibald retires from cycling after falling 'in love' with nursing
headlines.description: Three-time Olympic medallist Katie Archibald retires from cycling - despite being previously chosen for Scotland's 2026 Commonwealth Games team - having "fallen in love" with her new career as a nurse.

headlines.title: Forty years of the EFL play-offs - choose your favourite memories
headlines.description: BBC Sport looks back at 15 memorable moments from 40 years of the play-offs in the English Football League.

headlines.title: Southampton ask for more time over spying claims
headlines.description: Southampton request more time to conduct an internal review after being charged with spying on Championship play-off rivals Middlesbrough.

headlines.title: Where problems started with 2026 rules & calendar congestion - F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your latest questions.
